segments.text: Why do you stand far away, O LORD? Why do you hide yourself in times of trouble?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: In the pride of the wicked, the enemy pursues the afflicted;

footnotes: The wicked…: Heb. In the pride of the wicked he doth persecute

segments.text: For the wicked boasts of the desires of his soul,
and the one greedy for gain curses and renounces the LORD.
segments.speaker: Unknown

footnotes: blesseth…: or, the covetous blesseth himself, he abhorreth the LORD

segments.text: The wicked, in the pride of his face, does not seek him;
all his thoughts are, 'There is no God'.
segments.speaker: Wicked Person

footnotes: God is…: or, all his thoughts are, There is no God

segments.text: His ways prosper at all times; your judgments are on high, out of his sight; as for all his foes, he puffs at them.
He says in his heart, 'I shall not be moved; throughout all generations I shall not meet adversity.'
segments.speaker: Wicked Person

footnotes: never: Heb. unto generation and generation

segments.text: His mouth is filled with cursing and deceit and oppression; under his tongue are mischief and iniquity.
He sits in ambush in the villages; in hiding places he murders the innocent. His eyes stealthily watch for the helpless.
He lurks in ambush in secret like a lion in his thicket; he lurks that he may seize the poor; he seizes the poor when he draws him into his net.

footnotes: secretly: Heb. in the secret places

segments.text: The helpless are crushed, sink down, and fall by his might.

footnotes: by…: or, into his strong parts

segments.text: He says in his heart, 'God has forgotten, he has hidden his face, he will never see it.'
segments.speaker: Wicked Person

segments.text: Arise, O LORD; O God, lift up your hand; forget not the afflicted.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Why does the wicked renounce God and say in his heart, 'You will not call to account'?
But you do see, for you note mischief and vexation, that you may take it into your hands.
To you the helpless commits himself; you have been the helper of the fatherless.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Break the arm of the wicked and evildoer; call his wickedness to account till you find none.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: The LORD is king forever and ever; the nations perish from his land.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: O LORD, you hear the desire of the afflicted; you will strengthen their heart; you will incline your ear to do justice to the fatherless and the oppressed, so that man who is of the earth may strike terror no more.
segments.speaker: David
